_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: Local batch: 4 Orlando city commissioners (Keen D1, Ortiz D2, Chapin D3, Rose D5) evidence-finalized + 1 currency correction (Ings D6 -> former, replaced 2018 by Bakari Burns). Roster currency verified against orlando.gov: Keen sworn Jan 12 2026, Chapin sworn Jan 12 2026, Rose sworn Jun 24 2024 + re-elected Nov 4 2025, Ortiz since 2008 (term to Jan 10 2028). Orlando seats officially NONPARTISAN; actual party affiliation noted in profile.background but party field unchanged. All TRUE/FALSE cells cite a primary URL; vague slogans + party-default presumptions held null. NOTE FOR ADAM: Originally targeted rick-west (Mayor of Chesapeake VA, R) but slug 'rick-west' collides with a second OK House Rep candidate; refine-records.py picks the OK House record (later in the array). Chesapeake-Mayor research is preserved in refinements/research-notes-rick-west-chesapeake-2026-06-05.md for the next batch once the slug collision is patched (rename OK record to rick-west-ok-hd3 or similar).

profile.background: Charles Thomas 'Tom' Keen III. Orlando City Commissioner, District 1 (officially nonpartisan ballot); personal voter registration Democrat (previously Republican, party-switcher). Sworn in January 12, 2026 after defeating incumbent Jim Gray 53.97%-31.25% in November 4, 2025 election. Prior offices: Florida House District 35 (Jan 27, 2024 – Nov 5, 2024; lost rematch to Erika Booth 48.02%-51.98%); 2017 Orlando D1 race (lost to Gray); 2022 FL House D35 Democratic primary (lost by 57 votes); 2012 IN State Senate D16 (lost). Bachelor's degree, University of Cincinnati. U.S. Navy retired Commander (O-5); Naval Flight Officer designated 1983; airborne reconnaissance with shore tour at U.S. Embassy London. Post-military: manager at Collins Aerospace. Previously served on Orlando Citizens' Police Review Board and Mayor's Veterans Advisory Council. Current 4-year term: Jan 12, 2026 – Jan 2030.

tom-keen.notes_set: Orlando City Commissioner, District 1 (NP ballot; personal Democrat registration, party-switcher from R); sworn Jan 12, 2026, term to Jan 2030. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence pulled from his 2024 FL House campaign + 2025 D1 endorsement slate (only ~5 months in office, so no Orlando council roll-call yet on values cells): (a) endorsed by Florida Planned Parenthood; campaigned on 'restoring' abortion access and supported Amendment 4 (would have enshrined abortion to viability ~24 weeks in FL Constitution); criticized FL six-week ban — sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE; (b) endorsed by Equality Florida Action PAC in both 2024 House race and 2025 Orlando D1 race; self-described 'staunch advocate for LGBTQIA+ rights' — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE; (c) campaigned on 'keeping books on shelves' opposing library book removals; aligned with Eskamani / Carlos G. Smith on SOGI — family_child_sovereignty q2 FALSE; (d) endorsed by Giffords (gun-control PAC) in 2024 House race; supports universal background checks and strengthening FL's red-flag law — self_defense q1 FALSE. NULL on Christian liberty, economic stewardship (FY25-26 Orlando budget adopted Sept 2025 before he was seated; no FY27 vote yet), election integrity, public_justice (review-board service is oversight not affirmative backing), refuse_state_overreach, and border (Keen not individually quoted in Jan/Apr 2026 Orlando ICE-facility coverage). Religion not publicly stated.

1.note: Endorsed by Florida Planned Parenthood (2024 cycle); campaigned on 'restoring' abortion access and supported Amendment 4 (~24-week constitutional protection); criticized FL six-week ban.

4.note: Endorsed by Equality Florida Action PAC in both 2024 FL House race and 2025 Orlando D1 race; self-described 'staunch advocate for LGBTQIA+ rights.'

2.note: Campaigned on 'keeping books on shelves' opposing FL library book removals; aligned with Eskamani / Carlos G. Smith on SOGI/library content; opposes removal of SOGI/library materials.

1.note: Endorsed by Giffords (gun-control PAC) in 2024 FL House race; supports universal background checks and strengthening Florida's red-flag law.

profile.background: Antonio 'Tony' Ortiz. Orlando City Commissioner, District 2 (east Orlando / Azalea Park / Curry Ford / Lee Vista), since May 1, 2008; re-elected unopposed 2023; current term ends January 10, 2028. Officially nonpartisan; personal voter registration switched from Republican to Democrat on May 6, 2025 (cited GOP positions on LGBTQ rights, voting rights, immigration, climate); reportedly considering a 2027 Orlando mayoral run. Born Manatí, Puerto Rico; Orlando resident since 1989 — first/only Puerto Rican commissioner of City of Orlando. U.S. Marine Corps 1984, 9 years active+reserve, Persian Gulf War veteran. 14 years Orlando Police Department (Special Task Forces, Gangs Unit, Uniform Drug Unit, SWAT). Accounting/banking professional. Past President, Florida League of Cities. University of Central Florida.

tony-ortiz.notes_set: Orlando City Commissioner D2 since 2008 (NP ballot, personal voter reg R→D May 6, 2025); term to Jan 10, 2028; considering 2027 mayoral bid. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence: (a) lead author/champion of the 2018 Orlando Trust Act barring OPD from questioning immigration status in routine interactions; warned against 'inhumane collaboration with ICE'; in April 2026 joined commissioners opposing a proposed Orlando ICE facility ('Elections have consequences. And we are seeing real-life consequences.') — border_immigration q1 and q2 FALSE; (b) party-switch explicitly cited GOP 'efforts to target the LGBTQ+ community,' 'marginalization of Latinos and immigrants,' and 'suppress voting rights' — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE (oppositional posture to rubric-positive direction); (c) 14-year retired OPD officer (Gangs Unit, SWAT); platform and identity center law-enforcement service; no record of supporting defunding — public_justice q0 TRUE (career-identity + platform clears the affirmative-backing bar); (d) Orlando city millage held flat at 6.65 mills for 12 consecutive years and Ortiz has voted with that consensus — economic_stewardship q2 TRUE (caveat: 2014 he supported a one-time millage increase, but the 12-year flat-rate consensus record since dominates). NULL on sanctity_of_life (no city action; FL preemption), family/school (no city school authority; OCPS separate), Christian liberty (no public stance; religion not stated), election integrity (party-switch quote is slogan-level, no specific vote on Zuckerbucks/RCV/voter ID), refuse_state_overreach (no specific COVID/ESG/preemption vote located), and self_defense (FL 790.33 preempts city gun ordinances; no Ortiz-specific vote). Religion not publicly stated.

4.note: Publicly cited GOP 'efforts to target the LGBTQ+ community' as a reason for his May 2025 party switch from Republican to Democrat; serves on a council that has raised the Pride/trans flag at City Hall with no recorded dissent from him.

2.note: Orlando city millage held flat at 6.65 mills for 12 consecutive years; Ortiz has voted with that consensus throughout his tenure.

1.note: Lead author/champion of the 2018 Orlando Trust Act barring OPD from inquiring about immigration status; warned against 'inhumane collaboration with ICE'; April 2026 publicly opposed an Orlando ICE facility.

2.note: Championed Trust Act (sanctuary-style ordinance) and opposed local cooperation with federal immigration enforcement; April 2026 publicly opposed proposed Orlando ICE facility.

0.note: 14-year retired Orlando Police Department officer (Gangs Unit, SWAT, Special Task Forces); platform centers law-enforcement service; no record of supporting defund movement.

profile.background: Roger Elliot Chapin. Orlando City Commissioner, District 3 (officially nonpartisan ballot; personal voter registration Democrat per FL voter records). First D3 commissioner in 20 years; won December 9, 2025 runoff against Mira Tanna 3,884-3,684 (51.32%-48.68%) for seat held by Robert F. Stuart since 2006; sworn in January 12, 2026; current 4-year term runs to 2029. Profession: Public-affairs consultant (Chapin Communications, sole proprietor ~5 years); previously 20+ years at Mears Transportation rising to Executive Vice President (Mears remains his largest PR client and a city contractor). Mother is former Orange County Mayor Linda Chapin (D); College Park resident, lifelong Orlandoan; married 27 years to Susan Chicone Chapin. Prior civic boards: Orlando Utilities Commission; Chair, Downtown Development Board/CRA; Orlando Municipal Planning Board; Orlando Redistricting Advisory Board; Vice Chair, Orange County Charter Review Commission; Dr. Phillips Center Community Oversight Board; Orlando Health Foundation; Cure Sanfilippo Foundation; Goodwill Industries. 2025 endorsements: Mayor Buddy Dyer (D), outgoing Commissioner Robert Stuart (D), Orange County Sheriff John Mina (D), FOP Lodge 25, Orlando Professional Firefighters Local 1365, Orlando Sentinel, Orlando Regional Realtor Association, Central Florida Hotel & Lodging Association.

roger-chapin.notes_set: Orlando City Commissioner D3 (NP ballot, personal Democrat registration); sworn Jan 12, 2026 (first D3 commissioner in 20 years after Robert Stuart). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] ROSTER VERIFIED. SCORING: ALL CELLS NULL by evidence-first discipline — only ~5 months in office, the campaign was deliberately issue-light (platform listed only 'safer streets, reliable city services, and smart economic development'), and no public statement / council vote was located on any of the 10 LOCAL rubric categories. Notable context (not rubric-scored): (a) FOP Lodge 25 + Firefighters Local 1365 + Sheriff Mina endorsements signal public-safety alignment but no Chapin-attributable affirmative pro-police statement was located — the rubric high bar is HIS statement, not the FOP's; (b) opposed Gov. DeSantis's property-tax-elimination proposal as too blunt ('scalpel and not a machete'); has advocated 'expanding the TDT [Tourism Development Tax] to add transit and affordable housing benefits' — leans against economic_stewardship q2 but no actual budget/millage vote yet (FY27 Sept 2026 will produce the first); (c) consultants Consensus Communications (Tre' Evers) + Whitson Digital were Republican-aligned, but consultants are not the candidate; (d) son of former Orange County Mayor Linda Chapin (D). Religion / education not publicly stated. Recommend re-scoring after FY27 budget cycle (~Sept 2026) and any first-year proclamations.

profile.background: Shaniqua 'Shan' Rose. Orlando City Commissioner, District 5, since June 24, 2024 (took office via special runoff after Gov. DeSantis suspended prior Commissioner Regina Hill in April 2024 over elder-abuse/fraud allegations); re-elected Nov 4, 2025 defeating Hill ~52%-33% with Lawanna Gelser ~15%; current 4-year term runs to 2029. Officially nonpartisan ballot; actual party affiliation Democrat (filed 2022 Democratic primary for FL House District 41; FL Dem Party chair Nikki Fried publicly celebrated her 2025 win; endorsed by Run For Something). Born to a St. Lucian immigrant family, raised in Brooklyn NY; relocated to Central Florida 2000. Education: Poinciana HS; AA General Studies, Valencia College (2007); BA Supervision & Management, Polk State College (2015); MA Public Administration, American Public University (2018). Career: former City of Orlando staff in Economic Development (Downtown Development Board/CRA) and Permitting Office; founder/CEO of Change For The Community (CFTC, est. July 4, 2019); Executive Director of Eatonville CRA. Civic: NAACP, League of Women Voters, American Red Cross, National Congress of Black Women. Single mother; widow (lost husband to gun violence).

shaniqua-shan-rose.notes_set: Orlando City Commissioner D5 (NP ballot, actual Democrat); sworn June 24, 2024 (special), re-elected Nov 4, 2025; term to 2029. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence (mostly from 2022 HD41 Winter Garden Vox candidate questionnaire + 2024-25 council platform): (a) 2022 questionnaire — 'women should have access to reproductive healthcare and not be forced to give birth to children that might create a financial hardship for them' — sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE; (b) self-described 'survivor of gun violence' (lost husband); platform supports 'reduction of gun violence' and school-safety measures including metal detectors / standardized LE training / secure access points — self_defense q1 FALSE (gun-control orientation; FL 790.33 preempts city ordinances regardless); (c) Fraternal Order of Police endorsement; platform centers 'community policing'; took credit for '27% drop in crime' under her tenure — public_justice q0 TRUE (FOP endorsement + affirmative public backing clears high bar). NULL on biblical marriage, family/school (no city school authority), Christian liberty, economic stewardship (no individual Rose vote against the 12-year flat millage on record either way), election integrity, refuse_state_overreach, and border (Mayor Dyer not Rose handled the Jan 2026 ICE-facility statement). Religion not publicly stated. Active investigation flag (not rubric-scored): March 2026 OPD referred her for FDLE investigation over alleged use of anonymous private email accounts from City Hall network to send harassing messages to a Pulse survivor advocacy group; Regina Hill has also filed a Nov 2025 election-fraud lawsuit against Rose and the canvassing board (Rose called it 'frivolous'). Both pending; flagged as governance-conduct context only.

1.note: 2022 HD41 candidate questionnaire: 'women should have access to reproductive healthcare and not be forced to give birth to children that might create a financial hardship for them' — pro-choice; no pro-life city action.

Self-described 'survivor of gun violence' (lost husband); platform supports 'reduction of gun violence' and school-safety measures including metal detectors / standardized LE training / secure access points; orientation is gun-control, not pro-2A.

0.note: Endorsed by the Fraternal Order of Police; platform centers 'community policing'; publicly credited a '27% drop in crime' on her tenure; affirmative public backing of police clears the rubric's high bar.

profile.background: Samuel B. Ings — FORMER Orlando City Commissioner, District 6 (West Orlando — Pine Hills, parts of Parramore / Mercy Drive corridor). Elected 2006, re-elected 2010 and 2014. Did NOT seek re-election in 2018; ran for Mayor of Orlando in November 2018 (lost to incumbent Buddy Dyer; finished second behind Aretha Simons). Has not held elected office since. Bakari F. Burns has held Orlando D6 continuously since the 2018 election. Background: ~25+ years sworn officer with the Orlando Police Department, retired as sergeant before entering elected office. Small-business owner (private security / consulting) post-OPD. Officially nonpartisan ballot; personal voter registration Democrat. Publicly identifies as Christian; honored by predominantly Black Protestant churches in west Orlando across his career.

samuel-b-ings.notes_set: CURRENCY CORRECTION [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement]: Samuel B. Ings is NOT the sitting Orlando City Commissioner D6 as of June 2026. He served D6 2006-2018, then ran (and lost) for Mayor in November 2018. The current D6 commissioner is Bakari F. Burns (since 2018). Marking record `status: former`. The active Orlando D6 record should be created/refreshed under Bakari F. Burns; this Ings record should be archived. Verified via the official City of Orlando roster page (orlando.gov/Our-Government/Mayor-City-Council/) which lists Burns at D6 and contains no entry for Ings on the current council.
